content: Curiosity-driven autonomous agents can leverage intrinsic reward signals to dynamically prune and prioritize the exponentially large search space of Task and Motion Planning (TAMP) problems, eliminating the need for hand-specified goal priors that often fail in unstructured real-world environments. This curiosity-guided planning framework treats unmodeled geometric and interaction uncertainties in TAMP as exploratory opportunities for the agent to update its planning operator library on the fly.
